scale.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl tracking-tighter leading-none font-black
scale.h2: text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl tracking-tight leading-none font-bold
scale.label: text-xs tracking-[0.2em] uppercase font-bold

typography.pairing_strategy: Wide Display Sans for aggressive game-like headings (Unbounded). Sterile, mechanical Mono for the body, stats, and scores (JetBrains Mono). Text must feel like a high-end HUD.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Control Room layout tailored for split-screen intensity. Dense and functional, mapping the physical layout of an arcade cabinet mixed with a trading terminal.

grid_system.behavior: Left side is the Planner Core (The Jar). Right side is the Game Core (The Arena). In single-view mode, active core takes col-span-full.

buttons.primary_ship_it: bg-transparent border-2 border-[#FF00AA] text-[#FF00AA] hover:bg-[#FF00AA] hover:text-white shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(255,0,170,0.5)] transition-all duration-300 uppercase tracking-widest font-bold px-8 py-4 rounded-none
buttons.secondary: bg-transparent border border-[#00FFCC]/50 text-[#00FFCC] hover:bg-[#00FFCC]/10 hover:border-[#00FFCC] transition-all px-6 py-3 rounded-none text-sm uppercase tracking-widest

cards_and_panels.style: Retro-Futurism (Terminal Look). Black base, 1px cyan or pink borders, inner glow.
cards_and_panels.classes: bg-[#0A0A0A] border border-white/10 backdrop-blur-xl relative overflow-hidden

the_jar_simulation.style: Glassmorphism. Needs to look like a realistic 2D glass container.
the_jar_simulation.classes: border-x-2 border-b-2 border-white/20 bg-white/5 backdrop-blur-sm rounded-b-3xl shadow-[inset_0_-10px_30px_rgba(0,255,204,0.1)]

stones.low: bg-white/20 text-white text-xs border border-white/30 backdrop-blur-md

pong_arena.style: Classic arcade with a hyper-modern neon twist.

surfaces_and_textures.background_effect: CRT scanlines overlay using repeating-linear-gradient, plus subtle CSS noise. Do not use pure black without the faint cyan/pink ambient glows in corners.
surfaces_and_textures.glass_effects: Backdrop-blur-xl with backdrop-saturate-150. Border-white/10 on edges.

motion_and_interactions.physics_engine: Matter.js is highly recommended for the Jar to simulate weight, gravity, and bounds realistically.
motion_and_interactions.pong_engine: requestAnimationFrame with custom physics, bounding box collision, and velocity multipliers for combos.
motion_and_interactions.particles: Use canvas-based particles for 'Ship It!' bursts (pink/cyan sparks) and daily reset vaporization.
motion_and_interactions.hover_states: Buttons must have an aggressive hover state. E.g., instant background fill with heavy box-shadow glow.
motion_and_interactions.transitions: Use spring-based animations (Framer Motion) for UI panels. Snappy, rigid, tech-like (stiffness: 300, damping: 25).

images.description: Abstract grid pattern with warm and cool lighting. Use as a highly opaque (10-15%) overlay for the daily reset replay poster or the background body.
Motherboard circuits with a neon glow and diagonal grid overlay. Good for the 'Game Over' or 'Save the Day' snapshot modal background.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text over glowing buttons meets APCA standards. If text is black on a cyan button, it's fine. If text is cyan on black, ensure weight is bold enough.
accessibility.motion: Respect prefers-reduced-motion for the particle bursts and physics (allow fallback to simple list view if needed).
accessibility.test_attributes: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid. Examples: data-testid='ship-it-button', data-testid='task-input', data-testid='view-toggle'.

instructions_to_main_agent: Use standard JavaScript (.js/.jsx) with Tailwind CSS.
Install framer-motion for UI interactions (menus, modals, layout shifts).
Use matter-js for the 'Jar' physics simulation (stones dropping, colliding, resting). Provide a canvas overlay.
For the Pong arena, use a custom requestAnimationFrame loop on a 2D canvas.
Implement the 'Ship It!' flow: clicking the button removes a task from the Jar, fires a burst of particles, and triggers a ball speed multiplier in the Pong arena.
The Jar must visually indicate volume/capacity. If the simulated area of the bodies exceeds a threshold, spawn warning particles.
Ensure the split-screen layout adapts gracefully to mobile (stacking vertically, or providing a toggle between Planner and Arena).
